serviceDescription: Amazon DynamoDB is a key-value and document database that delivers single-digit millisecond performance at any scale. It's a fully managed, multiregion, multimaster, durable database with built-in security, backup and restore, and in-memory caching for internet-scale applications.

helpText.aws: DynamoDB offers two table classes designed to help you optimize for cost. The DynamoDB Standard table class is the default and recommended for the vast majority of workloads. The DynamoDB Standard-Infrequent Access (DynamoDB Standard-IA) table class is optimized for tables that store data that is accessed infrequently, where storage is the dominant cost. Each table class offers different pricing for data storage as well as read and write requests. You can select the most cost-effective table class based on your table’s storage requirements and data access patterns. Learn more about DynamoDB table classes in the DynamoDB Developer Guide.
helpText.aws-cn: The DynamoDB Standard table class is available
helpText.aws-iso: The DynamoDB Standard table class is available
helpText.aws-iso-b: The DynamoDB Standard table class is available

The DynamoDB Standard-Infrequent Access (DynamoDB Standard-IA) table class is optimized for tables that store data that is accessed infrequently, where storage is the dominant cost. Each table class offers different pricing for data storage as well as read and write requests. You can select the most cost-effective table class based on your table’s storage requirements and data access patterns. Learn more about DynamoDB table classes in the DynamoDB Developer Guide.

components.text: DynamoDB offers two table classes designed to help you optimize for cost. The DynamoDB Standard table class is the default and recommended for the vast majority of workloads. The DynamoDB Standard-Infrequent Access (DynamoDB Standard-IA) table class is optimized for tables that store data that is accessed infrequently, where storage is the dominant cost. Each table class offers different pricing for data storage as well as read and write requests.
The calculations in this section exclude AWS Free Tier discounts.
The calculations in this section exclude Amazon Web Services Free Tier discounts.

components.label: Data storage size
components.placeholder: Enter the total for all tables and indexes

components.text: Specify the write consistency of your workload.

components.label: Percentage of Non-transactional writes
components.description: Write operations create, update, or delete items.

components.label: Percentage of Transactional writes
components.description: Coordinated, all-or-nothing write operations that provide atomicity, consistency, isolation, and durability (ACID).

components.label: usage alert component
components.errorText: Percentage sum of Non-transactional writes and Transactional writes should be 100%

components.label: Baseline write rate
components.description: Enter the number of writes per second that your workload needs during off-peak periods.

components.label: Peak write rate
components.description: Enter the maximum number of writes per second that your workload needs during peak periods.
components.placeholder: Enter the maximum number of writes

components.label: usage alert component
components.errorText: Peak write rate must be greater than or equal to baseline write rate

components.label: Duration of peak write activity
components.description: Enter the number of hours per month that your write workload operates at peak.

components.label: Percentage of baseline writes covered by reserved capacity
components.description: The reserved capacity also applies to your peak write rate.

components.label: Percentage of baseline writes covered by reserved capacity
components.description: The reserved capacity also applies to your peak write rate.

components.content: Reserved Capacity is not available for the Standard-IA table class

components.text: The calculations in this section exclude AWS Free Tier discounts.
The calculations in this section exclude Amazon Web Services Free Tier discounts.
Specify the read consistency of your workload.

components.label: Eventually consistent percentage
components.description: Returns a response that might not reflect the results of a recently completed write operation.

components.label: Strongly consistent percentage
components.description: Returns a response with the most up-to-date data reflecting all prior successful write operations.

components.label: Transactional percentage
components.description: Enables coordinated, all-or-nothing read operations that provide atomicity, consistency, isolation, and durability (ACID).

components.label: usage alert component
components.errorText: Percentage sum of Eventually consistent reads, Strongly consistent reads and Transactional reads should not exceed 100%

components.label: Baseline read rate
components.description: Enter the number of reads per second that your workload needs during off-peak periods.

components.label: Peak read rate
components.description: Enter the maximum number of reads per second your workload needs during peak periods.
components.placeholder: Enter the maximum number of reads

components.label: usage alert component
components.errorText: Peak read rate must be greater than or equal to baseline read rate

components.label: Duration of peak read activity
components.description: Enter the number of hours per month when your read workload operates at peak.

components.label: Percentage of baseline reads covered by reserved capacity
components.description: The reserved capacity also applies to your peak read rate.

components.label: Percentage of baseline writes covered by reserved capacity
components.description: The reserved capacity also applies to your peak write rate.

components.label: info alert
components.content: Reserved Capacity is not available for the Standard-IA table class

components.label: price for provisioned read capacity units
